page: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Utilizing the ARM64 architecture, this instance features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with a single thread per core. The processor is equipped with 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance and multi-core efficiency. The instance achieves top-tier status in Passmark memory testing and large-block memory bandwidth, with read speeds reaching 397.95 GB/sec and write speeds at 191.37 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is highly competitive, as evidenced by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score and strong bzip3 multi-threaded decompression throughput of 360.4 GB/sec. Database operations also perform exceptionally well, scoring in the top tier for Passmark database operations and demonstrating strong throughput in Redis SET operations. Single-core performance and LLM inference speeds generally fall within the average to strong range, though processing large language models exhibits weaker prompt processing speeds.

The ARM64-based Graviton architecture offers a highly qualitative cost-efficient profile, delivering high memory bandwidth and strong multi-threaded performance without the overhead of traditional x86 architectures. This makes the m8g.8xlarge well-suited for memory-bound applications, high-throughput database operations, and web serving. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which should be factored into deployment architectures. Recommended workloads include static web serving, multi-threaded data compression, database hosting, and general-purpose containerized applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the ARM64-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs at 2.8 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 397.95 GB/sec and strong multi-core processing capabilities. With 4.0 GB of RAM per core and a 36 MB L3 cache, it balances compute and memory resources efficiently. The custom ARM architecture offers a highly cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads and memory-intensive tasks. This instance is highly suited for database hosting, web serving, and general-purpose containerized applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated physical cores, 128 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores
Equipped with 128 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark performance
Baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database hosting, multi-threaded decompression, and web serving
